rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Req. For Extension Of Time To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Nov. 28, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR EXTENSION OF TIME TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR, dismissal on file. = (501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MEERA BALAKUMAR VS. EMMA FAHY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Nov. 28, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part, and moot in part. Motion is granted as to the request for attorney's fees without leave to amend. This request appears to be unopposed. Motion is granted as to the request for punitive damages with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of malice, oppression or fraud. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to the request to remove the defendant from the premises. Plaintiff shall allege facts and the basis for such request. The Court notes again that there are no cases cited in opposition to support the availability of such relief under the law. Motion is otherwise moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. = (501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MEERA BALAKUMAR VS. EMMA FAHY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Nov. 28, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurer is overruled as to the cause of action for nuisance. This cause of action is sufficiently alleged. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to all other causes of action for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts (no conclusions) in support of each element of each cause of action. The Court notes that the opposition contained very few cases and no cases at all with similar facts. = (501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ROGER STERN VS. LENA GIKKAS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Nov. 28, 2023 line 9. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. As to the causes of action for breach of express and implied contract, quiet title and constructive trust (remedy) Cross-Complainant shall allege the nature of the contract - oral, written, express, implied, etc. and the exact terms of the contract outlining the rights and obligations of each party to the contract, the performance by one party and the breach by the other; all elements of the cause of action for quiet title, including, but not limited to as to the date of quieting title. Plaintiff shall also allege facts showing compliance with the Statute of Frauds or exception therefrom. The Court notes that Cross-Complainant fails to cite any authorities for the apparent proposition that Statute of Frauds does not apply to partnerships/joint ventures regarding real property. As to the cause for action for fraud Cross-Complainant shall allege each element of this cause of action with specificity (who, when, how, in what manner, etc). As to the cause of action for Negligent Infliction Emotional Distress Cross-Complainant appears to suggest that this cause of action requires "physical injury or impact," but does not point to any allegations supporting such claim in the operative Cross-Complaint. (Opposition 12:20-23). Cross-Complainant shall have leave to amend to make such allegations. The Court notes that there were additional grounds for demurrer (e,g. CC 47(b), etc). The Court does not reach those issues, and will address them if necessary upon the filing of the Amended Cross-Complaint. Cross-Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. The Court takes notice of the existence of the original and amended complaint in this action; as well as of the existence of a related proceedings. = (501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
